content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer XXX. You can perform the following two types of operations on it any number of times(possibly, zero):


Choose a positive integer KKK, and then multiply XXX with KKK.
If XXX is a perfect square, change XXX to X\sqrt{X}X​.
Find the minimum value of XXX you can achieve.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, an integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line - the minimum possible value of XXX

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: - The only positive integers smaller than 333 are 111 and 222. Since it is not possible to make XXX equal to 111 or 444, therefore, 333 is the answer.
Test Case 333: - One of the ways to convert 404040 to 101010 is given below-

Multiply XXX by 101010, the value of XXX is now 400400400
Convert XXX to X\sqrt{X}X​, the value of XXX is 202020
Multiply XXX by 555, the value of XXX is now 100100100.
Convert XXX to X\sqrt{X}X​, the value of XXX is 101010
It can be proved that this is the minimum possible value for the given XXX.
